channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo-Uganda a Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak spreading between Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, with at least 88 suspected deaths. No approved vaccines exist for this strain.

item.title: Ukraine Launches Massive Drone Attack on Moscow, Killing Four
item.description: Ukraine launched around 600 drones at Russia in one of the largest attacks on Moscow since the war began, killing at least four people including an Indian worker. Zelenskyy called it a justified response to Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.

item.title: Hamas Gaza Commander Killed by Israel, Testing Ceasefire
item.description: Israel assassinated Hamas top commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza. The group pledged to adapt operations following the killing, which comes amid ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: Bond Markets Signal New Era of Elevated Yields Amid War-Driven Inflation
item.description: US bond traders see a potential shift toward sustained higher borrowing costs as war-driven inflation fears intensify. The trend marks a departure from decades of declining yields.

item.title: US to Weigh Action Against China Over Industrial Overcapacity
item.description: US Trade Ambassador says President Trump will be presented with options if investigations determine Chinese industrial overcapacity harms American interests, as trade tensions persist.

item.title: Indonesia Pushes Back Against Chinese Business Complaints Over New Rules
item.description: Chinese businesses issued a blunt warning to President Prabowo that tougher regulations are hurting investor confidence, revealing tensions between Jakarta push for resource control and foreign capital.

item.title: Russian Drone Barrage Kills 2, Injures 41 Across Ukraine
item.description: Russia launched 287 attack drones overnight, with Ukraine shooting down 279. Eight drones struck seven locations in the latest escalation of aerial attacks.

item.title: White House to Host Mass Prayer Event Promoting Christian Origins of US
item.description: Trump administration officials including Pete Hegseth, Marco Rubio, and Mike Johnson will address a mass prayer meeting in Washington as part of US 250th anniversary celebrations, drawing criticism from those who see it as Christian nationalism.

item.title: ASML-Tata Sign Semiconductor Deal During Modi Netherlands Visit
item.description: India Prime Minister Modi witnessed the signing of a deal between Dutch tech giant ASML and Tata Electronics to build and scale a semiconductor plant in Gujarat, advancing India chip manufacturing ambitions.

item.title: US Assesses Drone Threat From Cuba, Report Says
item.description: Cuba has acquired more than 300 military drones and is discussing plans to use them against the US base at Guantanamo Bay, according to a new assessment raising security concerns.

item.title: China Signals Tariff Cuts After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: China and the US agreed to expand agricultural trade through tariff reductions and address non-tariff barriers following the Beijing summit. The agreement marks a potential de-escalation in trade tensions between the two largest economies.

item.title: South Korea Pursues All Options to Prevent Samsung Strike
item.description: South Korea's prime minister said the government is considering all measures to prevent a potential Samsung Electronics strike that could cause unimaginable economic damage. Samsung accounts for nearly a quarter of South Korea's exports.

item.title: Drone Strike Causes Fire at UAE Barakah Nuclear Plant
item.description: A drone strike targeted the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in Abu Dhabi, causing a fire at an electrical generator outside the inner perimeter. No injuries or radiation risks were reported, and the IAEA confirmed normal radiation levels.

item.title: Italian PM Meloni Visits Modena After Car-Ramming Incident
item.description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni travelled to Modena after a driver with mental health problems rammed into pedestrians. Several people were injured, and Meloni cancelled a Cyprus meeting to visit the victims.

item.title: Tata-ASML Partnership to Build India's First Semiconductor Fab
item.description: Tata Electronics and Dutch chip equipment maker ASML signed an agreement to build India's first front-end semiconductor fabrication plant in Gujarat, marking a major milestone in the country's push for chip manufacturing independence.

item.title: Live Updates: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Iran Says It Cannot Trust Americans
item.description: CBS News provides ongoing coverage of the Middle East conflict as Israel continues strikes on Lebanon and Iran declares it cannot trust the US, while FIFA officials hold constructive talks with Iranian federation about World Cup participation.

item.title: US Army leaders questioned over Poland deployment cancellation
item.description: Lawmakers grilled Army officials over the timing and reasons behind a cancelled Poland deployment, with Rep. Don Bacon calling it a "terrible signal" to NATO allies at a critical moment for European security.

item.title: NATO tests ground combat robots near Russian border in Latvia
item.description: NATO forces exercised in Latvia's dense forests this week, testing ground robots in combat scenarios near the Russian border as the alliance explores autonomous warfare capabilities.

item.title: Macron Opens Africa Summit in Kenya With $27 Billion Investment Package
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced €23 billion in investments at the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, marking France's first major summit in an Anglophone African country. The move signals a shift from colonial-era dominance toward "partnership of equals" as France seeks new allies.

item.title: Coal Makes Global Comeback as Iran War Cuts Off LNG Supplies
item.description: Countries worldwide are returning to coal power after the Iran war effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, cutting off about 20% of global liquefied natural gas supplies. The shift is most pronounced in Asia as nations prioritize energy security over climate goals.

item.title: Iran stock market to reopen Tuesday after conflict suspension
item.description: Iran will reopen its stock market on Tuesday following a suspension during the conflict with the US and Israel, IRNA news agency reported. The market closure was one of the economic impacts of the Gulf War.

item.title: Palestinian President Abbas son elected to top Fatah leadership body
item.description: Yasser Abbas, the 64-year-old son of aging Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas, won a seat on Fatahs central committee in elections seen as a potential succession move. The vote comes amid broader Palestinian political uncertainty.

item.title: Poland blindsided by email glitch on US troop movements
item.description: Poland's military chief received alerts about US troop relocations via email, revealing coordination failures as American forces shift. The incident raises questions about NATO communication protocols.

item.title: Tens of thousands protest in London across two major demonstrations
item.description: Massive crowds marched through central London on Saturday in separate protests over immigration policy and economic conditions, highlighting growing social tensions in Britain.

item.title: Singapore deepfake scam steals $3.8 million via fake PM video call
item.description: A sophisticated scam using deepfake technology to impersonate Singapore Prime Minister Wong and President Tharman in a Zoom call conned a victim out of millions. Police have obtained footage of the conference.
